title: 7 Best Herbal Teas For Hypertension
intro: Herbal teas for hypertension are a natural remedy made from various plants and herbs that help to lower blood pressure and alleviate symptoms of hypertension. These teas work by relaxing blood vessels, reducing stress and anxiety, and regulating heart rate, which in turn helps to decrease blood pressure levels. Examples of herbal teas that can help treat hypertension include chamomile tea, which has anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe blood vessels; hawthorn tea, which helps to strengthen heart muscles and improve blood flow; dandelion tea, which acts as a diuretic to reduce fluid retention; and peppermint tea, which helps to reduce stress and anxiety. Additionally, other herbal teas such as ginger tea, which helps to reduce inflammation and improve blood flow, and passionflower tea, which acts as a natural sedative to calm the nervous system, can also be used to help manage hypertension.

preparations.herb_desc: Valeriana officinalis teas, also known as valerian root tea, have been traditionally used to treat hypertension due to its sedative and relaxing properties. The herbal preparation helps to reduce blood pressure by promoting relaxation and reducing stress, which are key factors in managing hypertension. The bioactive constituents of Valeriana officinalis, such as valerenic acid, isovalerenol, and valeranone, contribute to its therapeutic effects by acting as GABA receptor agonists, which helps to calm the nervous system and lower blood pressure. Regular consumption of Valeriana officinalis teas can provide numerous benefits, including reduced anxiety and stress levels, improved sleep quality, and a decrease in systolic and diastolic blood pressure readings.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of boiling water, 2-3 teaspoons of dried Valeriana officinalis root, and a tea infuser or strainer.
Measure 2-3 teaspoons of dried Valeriana officinalis root and place it in a tea infuser or strainer.
Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the Valeriana officinalis root in the tea infuser or strainer.
Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes. Strain the tea into a cup and discard the solids.
Drink 1 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day, as needed, to help manage hypertension. Consult a doctor before using Valeriana officinalis for medicinal purposes.
preparations.preparation_study: According to the study, Valeriana officinalis teas for hypertension may be effective due to its vasorelaxant activity, which was observed in 44% of the plants examined, including Valeriana officinalis, showing more than 50% inhibition in isolated rat aortic rings.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Valeriana officinalis teas can help alleviate hypertension symptoms, but they may cause side effects such as dizziness, headaches, and stomach upset due to their sedative properties, which can also interact with certain medications like blood thinners and sedatives. When using Valeriana officinalis teas to treat hypertension, it's essential to start with small doses and gradually increase as needed, avoid combining with other sedatives or stimulants, and be cautious when driving or operating heavy machinery due to potential drowsiness.
preparation_usage: Consult doctor before using Valeriana officinalis for hypertension treatment.
Start with 250mg tea, 2 times a day, for 1 week to monitor body's response.
Gradually increase dose to 500mg, 2 times a day, if needed after 1 week.
Monitor blood pressure regularly and adjust dose as per doctor's advice.
Use Valeriana officinalis tea for 2-3 months, then reassess and adjust treatment plan.

preparations.herb_desc: Passiflora incarnata teas, also known as maypop or passionflower, have been traditionally used to treat hypertension due to their sedative and vasodilatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Passiflora incarnata, including flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, help to reduce blood pressure by relaxing blood vessels and improving cardiovascular function. The herbal preparation has been shown to help treat hypertension by reducing anxiety, stress, and inflammation, which are common contributing factors to high blood pressure. Regular consumption of Passiflora incarnata teas may also help to improve sleep quality, reduce symptoms of insomnia, and promote overall cardiovascular well-being, making it a beneficial herbal remedy for managing hypertension.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried Passiflora incarnata flowers.
Measure 1 cup of boiling water in a teapot.
Add the 2 tablespoons of dried flowers to the boiling water.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes, then strain the tea.
Drink 1 cup of the tea, 2-3 times a day, as needed to help manage hypertension.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Passiflora incarnata teas can be effective in treating hypertension, but some users may experience side effects such as dizziness, headaches, and nausea due to the herbal preparation's ability to lower blood pressure. To minimize potential risks, it is recommended to start with a low dose and gradually increase it, avoid using Passiflora incarnata teas with other medications that also lower blood pressure, and be cautious when consuming them with food or before engaging in physical activities.
preparation_usage: Consult a doctor before using Passiflora incarnata tea for hypertension
Start with 1 teaspoon of dried herb in 1 cup of boiling water, 2 times a day
Gradually increase the dose to 2-3 teaspoons in 1 cup of boiling water, 2 times a day
Monitor blood pressure regularly and adjust the dose as advised by your doctor
Continue using the tea for 3-6 months under your doctor's guidance

preparations.herb_desc: Lavandula angustifolia teas have been traditionally used to treat hypertension due to their vasodilatory and anxiolytic properties, which help to reduce blood pressure and alleviate symptoms of anxiety. The bioactive constituents present in Lavandula angustifolia teas, including linalool and linalyl acetate, have been shown to exert a calming effect on the nervous system, thereby reducing the fight-or-flight response that can contribute to increased blood pressure. Regular consumption of Lavandula angustifolia teas has been found to help lower blood pressure in individuals with hypertension by improving cardiovascular function and reducing oxidative stress. The benefits of using Lavandula angustifolia teas to treat hypertension include reduced risk of cardiovascular disease, improved sleep quality, and a decrease in the need for antihypertensive medications.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Lavandula angustifolia flowers or 2 tablespoons of dried flowers.
Measure 1 cup of boiling water and pour it over the flowers in a heat-resistant cup.
Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes. Strain the tea if using fresh flowers.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey to taste, if needed. Honey is a natural sweetener.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help manage hypertension. Consult a doctor before using herbal remedies.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Lavandula angustifolia teas can potentially help lower blood pressure due to its calming and relaxing properties, but consuming it may cause side effects such as dizziness, headaches, and nausea, especially in people with pre-existing conditions or when taken in large quantities. To minimize the risks, it is recommended to start with a small dose and gradually increase the amount, as well as avoid consuming it before driving or operating heavy machinery to prevent accidents.
preparation_usage: Consult a doctor before using Lavandula angustifolia tea for hypertension.
Drink 1 cup of Lavandula angustifolia tea 2-3 times a day.
Start with 1/2 teaspoon of dried flowers per cup of boiling water.
Gradually increase dosage to 1 teaspoon per cup as needed and under doctor's guidance.
Monitor blood pressure regularly and adjust dosage or consult doctor if needed.

preparations.herb_desc: Ginkgo biloba teas have been traditionally used to treat hypertension, a condition characterized by high blood pressure. The properties of this herbal preparation, including its vasodilatory and antioxidant effects, help to reduce blood pressure by improving blood flow and reducing oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Ginkgo biloba, such as flavonoids, terpenoids, and bilobalide, have been identified as key players in its ability to treat hypertension, as they help to relax blood vessels and improve cardiovascular function. The benefits of Ginkgo biloba teas in treating hypertension include reduced risk of cardiovascular disease, improved blood vessel function, and a decrease in symptoms such as headaches and dizziness.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of boiling water and 1 tablespoon of dried Ginkgo biloba leaves.
Measure 1 tablespoon of dried Ginkgo biloba leaves and place them in a tea infuser or a small muslin bag.
Pour the boiling water over the Ginkgo biloba leaves in the tea infuser or muslin bag.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes, then remove the Ginkgo biloba leaves from the water.
Drink the tea immediately and repeat the process 2-3 times a day as needed to help manage hypertension.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Clinical and experimental pharmacology & physiology", Ginkgo biloba teas for hypertension may be effective in attenuating the development of hypertension and inhibiting the increase in heart weight associated with sustained high blood pressure.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Ginkgo biloba teas can be used to help alleviate hypertension symptoms, but potential side effects may include stomach upset, nausea, and dizziness due to its blood-thinning properties, as well as interactions with blood pressure medications. Precautions to take when using Ginkgo biloba teas for hypertension treatment include being aware of potential allergic reactions, monitoring blood sugar levels, and avoiding concurrent use with antidepressants, antacids, and blood thinners to minimize risks.
preparation_usage: Consult a doctor before using Ginkgo biloba tea for hypertension.
Take 1-2 teaspoons of dried Ginkgo biloba leaves per cup of boiling water.
Steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain and discard the leaves.
Drink 1-2 cups of Ginkgo biloba tea 2-3 times a day, as directed by your doctor.
Monitor your blood pressure regularly and adjust your dosage as needed.

preparations.herb_desc: Panax ginseng teas have been traditionally used to treat hypertension, a condition characterized by elevated blood pressure, due to their ability to promote cardiovascular health and reduce stress levels. The properties of Panax ginseng teas that contribute to this effect include their antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and adaptogenic properties, which help to regulate blood pressure and improve overall cardiovascular function. The bioactive constituents of Panax ginseng teas, such as ginsenosides, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, are responsible for these beneficial effects by exerting vasodilatory and anti-hypertensive actions, thereby reducing blood pressure and improving cardiovascular health. The benefits of using Panax ginseng teas to treat hypertension include reduced risk of cardiovascular disease, improved mood and cognitive function, and a lower risk of complications associated with high blood pressure.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 teaspoon of dried Panax ginseng root and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the dried Panax ginseng root in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth.
Add honey or lemon to taste, if desired. Stir well to combine.
Drink 1-2 cups of the Panax ginseng tea per day, ideally 2-3 times a week, as directed by a healthcare professional or as needed for hypertension.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "The American journal of Chinese medicine", Panax ginseng teas for hypertension may improve vascular endothelial dysfunction by increasing the synthesis of nitric oxide in patients with hypertension.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Panax ginseng teas can help alleviate hypertension symptoms, but potential side effects may include insomnia, restlessness, and digestive issues, such as stomach upset and diarrhea. To minimize the risk of these side effects, it is recommended to start with a low dose and gradually increase as needed, and to avoid consuming the tea in the evening to prevent disrupting sleep patterns.
preparation_usage: Consult a doctor before using Panax ginseng tea for hypertension treatment.
Drink 1 cup of Panax ginseng tea 1-2 times a day.
Start with 1/4 teaspoon of ginseng per cup of boiling water.
Gradually increase the ginseng to 1/2 teaspoon after 2 weeks.
Monitor your blood pressure and adjust the dosage under doctor's guidance.

preparations.herb_desc: Crataegus monogyna teas have been traditionally used to treat hypertension due to their vasodilatory and antioxidant properties, which help to lower blood pressure and reduce oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of this herbal preparation, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenes, have been shown to help alleviate hypertension by inhibiting the activity of angiotensin-converting enzyme and improving blood vessel function. Regular consumption of Crataegus monogyna teas has been reported to help reduce systolic and diastolic blood pressure, improve cardiovascular health, and enhance overall well-being. The benefits of using Crataegus monogyna teas to treat hypertension include a reduced risk of cardiovascular disease, improved blood flow, and a natural alternative to conventional medications.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried Crataegus monogyna leaves and flowers. You can buy them at a health food store or online.
Use 1 cup of boiling water to make the tea. Pour the boiling water over the dried herbs in a cup or teapot.
Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes. This allows the herbs to release their active ingredients.
Strain the tea into another cup using a tea strainer or a piece of cheesecloth. Discard the solids.
Drink 1-2 cups of the tea per day, preferably after meals, to help manage hypertension. Consult with a healthcare professional before using Crataegus monogyna tea for medicinal purposes.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Crataegus monogyna teas can be effective in lowering blood pressure to treat hypertension, but possible side effects may include gastrointestinal issues such as nausea and diarrhea, as well as allergic reactions like skin rashes and itching. To use Crataegus monogyna teas safely, it is recommended to start with small doses and gradually increase as needed, and to avoid using it for prolonged periods of time as it may lead to electrolyte imbalance and kidney problems.
preparation_usage: Consult a doctor before using Crataegus monogyna tea for hypertension.
Drink 1 cup of Crataegus monogyna tea, 2-3 times a day.
Start with 1 teaspoon of dried leaves per cup, adjust to 2 teaspoons if needed.
Continue using Crataegus monogyna tea for 2-6 weeks to see noticeable results.
Monitor blood pressure regularly and adjust usage as directed by your doctor.

preparations.herb_desc: Melissa officinalis teas, also known as lemon balm tea, have been traditionally used to treat hypertension due to their vasodilatory and anxiolytic properties, which help to relax blood vessels and reduce blood pressure. This herbal preparation helps to treat hypertension by reducing anxiety and stress levels, promoting relaxation, and improving sleep quality, ultimately leading to a decrease in blood pressure. The bioactive constituents of Melissa officinalis teas, including rosmarinic acid, flavonoids, and terpenes, contribute to their therapeutic effects by inhibiting the activity of enzymes involved in blood pressure regulation and reducing the production of stress hormones. The benefits of using Melissa officinalis teas to treat hypertension include reduced blood pressure, improved sleep quality, and a decrease in the risk of cardiovascular disease.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Melissa officinalis leaves or 1 tablespoon of dried leaves.
Heat 1 cup of boiling water.
Add the Melissa officinalis leaves to the hot water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and discard the leaves. Add honey or lemon to taste.
Drink 1-2 cups of Melissa officinalis tea per day, as directed by a healthcare professional for hypertension.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Melissa officinalis teas can be effective in treating hypertension, but it may cause side effects such as dizziness, nausea, and headaches in some individuals due to its sedative and antispasmodic properties. To avoid these side effects, it is essential to start with a low dose and gradually increase the amount, as well as to be mindful of potential interactions with other medications, and to avoid taking it before driving or operating heavy machinery.
preparation_usage: Consult a doctor before using Melissa officinalis for hypertension treatment.
Start with 1/2 teaspoon of dried Melissa officinalis per cup of hot water 2-3 times a day.
Gradually increase to 1 teaspoon per cup if needed and under doctor's guidance.
Drink 1-2 cups of Melissa officinalis tea in the morning and evening for best results.
Monitor blood pressure regularly and adjust dosage as advised by your doctor.

preparations.herb_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis teas, also known as rosemary tea, have been traditionally used to help treat hypertension by its vasodilatory, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The herbal preparation helps to treat hypertension by reducing blood pressure, improving circulation, and mitigating oxidative stress. Bioactive constituents such as carnosic acid, rosmarinic acid, and camphor contribute to the tea's therapeutic effects, which include inhibiting the activity of angiotensin-converting enzyme and enhancing nitric oxide production. Regular consumption of Rosmarinus officinalis teas may help to reduce the risk of cardiovascular complications associated with hypertension, making it a potential complementary remedy for managing this condition.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of boiling water and 2-3 sprigs of fresh Rosmarinus officinalis leaves.
Take the sprigs and chop them into small pieces.
Steep the chopped leaves in boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the liquid into a cup and discard the leaves.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help manage hypertension.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Rosmarinus officinalis teas can be used to treat hypertension, but possible side effects may include stomach upset, dizziness, and interactions with medications such as blood thinners and high blood pressure medications. Precautions should be taken to avoid consuming large amounts, as this may cause an increase in blood pressure, and it's also essential to monitor blood pressure levels closely when using Rosmarinus officinalis teas to treat hypertension.

preparations.herb_desc: Zingiber officinale teas, derived from the rhizome of the ginger plant, have been traditionally used to treat hypertension, a condition characterized by elevated blood pressure. The bioactive constituents of these teas, including gingerol, shogaol, and gingerdiol, exhibit vasodilatory and antioxidant properties that help to reduce blood pressure and alleviate symptoms of hypertension. By relaxing blood vessels and improving cardiovascular health, Zingiber officinale teas can help to lower blood pressure and reduce the risk of related complications, such as heart disease and stroke. The benefits of using Zingiber officinale teas to treat hypertension include improved cardiovascular health, reduced risk of cardiovascular disease, and a natural alternative to conventional medications, making it a popular choice for those seeking a holistic approach to managing blood pressure.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 teaspoon of dried Zingiber officinale root (ginger) and 1 cup of boiling water.
Pour boiling water over ginger and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture into a cup and discard the ginger.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey (optional) to the tea and mix well.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day, or as directed by a healthcare professional, to help manage hypertension.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Natural products and bioprospecting, Zingiber officinale teas have been identified as a potential herbal remedy for hypertension, although the specific study does not provide direct information on its effects, it is mentioned as one of the medicinal herbs used to normalize hypertension.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Zingiber officinale teas can be used to treat hypertension, but its use may lead to side effects such as dizziness, nausea, and stomach upset in some individuals. When using Zingiber officinale teas to treat hypertension, precautions should be taken to avoid excessive consumption, which can cause interactions with other medications and worsen certain health conditions, such as bleeding disorders and low blood pressure.

preparations.herb_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas have been traditionally used to treat hypertension due to their potent bioactive constituents, including glycyrrhizin, flavonoids, and triterpenoids. These compounds exhibit vasodilatory properties, which help to reduce blood pressure by relaxing the smooth muscles of blood vessels and improving blood flow. The regular consumption of Glycyrrhiza glabra teas may help to alleviate hypertension by reducing the risk of cardiovascular complications and promoting overall cardiovascular health. The benefits of using Glycyrrhiza glabra teas to treat hypertension include reduced systolic and diastolic blood pressure, improved cardiovascular function, and a lower risk of hypertension-related complications.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Glycyrrhiza glabra root. Choose organic and high-quality roots.
Measure 1 tablespoon of the root and chop it into small pieces. You can also use 2-3 teaspoons of dried root.
Combine the chopped root with 1 cup of boiling water in a teapot. Let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the root. You can add honey or lemon to taste, but avoid adding sugar.
Drink 1-2 cups of the tea per day, preferably in the morning and afternoon. Consult with a healthcare professional before making any changes to your diet or medication routine.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Nederlands tijdschrift voor geneeskunde, Glycyrrhiza glabra teas for hypertension can cause hypokalaemia and persistent hypertension, even after discontinuation of conventional medication and potassium supplementation, if consumed excessively, such as in the case of drinking at least 3 litres of liquorice tea a day.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas can be used to treat hypertension, but they may cause side effects such as water retention, high blood pressure, and low levels of potassium in the body due to the presence of glycyrrhizin, which can interfere with the body's ability to regulate electrolytes. To use Glycyrrhiza glabra teas safely, it is essential to limit their consumption to no more than 1-2 cups per day and to drink plenty of water to help minimize the risk of these side effects.

preparations.herb_desc: Rauvolfia serpentina teas have been traditionally used to treat hypertension, a condition characterized by elevated blood pressure. The herbal preparation's properties, including its alkaloid content, help to reduce blood pressure by promoting vasodilation and reducing sympathetic nervous system activity, thereby alleviating hypertension symptoms. Bioactive constituents such as ajmaline, serpentine, and reserpine in Rauvolfia serpentina teas contribute to their blood pressure-lowering effects, making them a valuable natural remedy for hypertension treatment. The benefits of using Rauvolfia serpentina teas to treat hypertension include reduced risk of cardiovascular disease, improved cardiovascular health, and a safer alternative to conventional antihypertensive medications.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 teaspoon of dried Rauvolfia serpentina root powder and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the powder in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey (optional) to the tea and mix well.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day, or as directed by a healthcare professional.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Natural products and bioprospecting, Rauvolfia serpentina teas for hypertension have been found to have anti-hypertensive effects, although it is not specifically mentioned in the study, the review suggests that Rauvolfia serpentina is one of the medicinal herbs used to normalize hypertension.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Rauvolfia serpentina teas can be effective in treating hypertension, but possible side effects include drowsiness, dizziness, and changes in heart rate and blood pressure. Precautions should be taken when using this herbal preparation, such as limiting its use to short-term periods, avoiding driving or operating heavy machinery, and monitoring kidney function due to potential effects on kidney health.

preparations.herb_desc: Achillea millefolium teas, also known as yarrow tea, have been traditionally used to treat hypertension due to their vasodilatory properties. These properties help to relax blood vessels and improve blood flow, thereby reducing blood pressure. The bioactive constituents, including flavonoids and terpenoids, found in Achillea millefolium teas are responsible for their medicinal effects, including antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities that contribute to their ability to treat hypertension. Drinking Achillea millefolium tea regularly may help to reduce the risk of cardiovascular disease associated with hypertension, making it a beneficial herbal remedy for managing this condition.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Achillea millefolium leaves and flowers.
Measure 1 tablespoon of the dried leaves and flowers into a heat-resistant cup.
Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the dried leaves and flowers.
Let it steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the liquid into another cup.
Drink 1 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day, ideally after meals, to help manage hypertension.
preparations.preparation_study: According to the study, there are first results suggesting antihypertensive activities of Achillea millefolium teas, but further research is needed for confirmation.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Achillea millefolium teas can be used to treat hypertension, but possible side effects include stomach upset, dizziness, and allergic reactions in some individuals. Precautions when using Achillea millefolium teas to treat hypertension include monitoring blood pressure closely, avoiding excessive consumption, and being aware of interactions with other medications, such as blood thinners and medications for diabetes or high blood pressure.

preparations.herb_desc: Salvia officinalis teas have been traditionally used to treat hypertension due to their vasorelaxant, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of this herbal preparation, including rosmarinic acid, carnosic acid, and ursolic acid, help to reduce blood pressure by inhibiting the angiotensin-converting enzyme (ACE) and improving vascular function. This herbal preparation helps to treat hypertension by reducing the strain on blood vessels, improving circulation, and promoting relaxation, ultimately leading to a decrease in blood pressure. The benefits of Salvia officinalis teas in treating hypertension include reduced risk of cardiovascular disease, improved overall health, and a natural alternative to conventional medications.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Salvia officinalis leaves. Wash them with clean water.
Dry the leaves for 30 minutes. You can use a paper towel or let them air dry.
Measure 1 tablespoon of dried leaves and put them in a cup.
Add 1 cup of boiling water to the cup with the dried leaves. Let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and drink it. You can add honey or lemon to taste.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Acta physiologica et pharmacologica Bulgarica, Salvia officinalis teas for hypertension may be effective in lowering blood pressure due to the aqueous-alcohol extract causing moderate but prolonged lowering of the blood pressure in cats when applied intravenously and duodenally.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Salvia officinalis teas can be used to help lower blood pressure and treat hypertension, but possible side effects may include dizziness, headaches, and stomach upset due to the high levels of potassium and other minerals. To use Salvia officinalis teas safely, it's essential to start with a low dose and gradually increase it to avoid over-exertion, and to also monitor your potassium levels and blood pressure regularly, as excessive consumption can lead to potassium overload and other complications.

preparations.herb_desc: Camellia sinensis teas, derived from the leaves of the Camellia sinensis plant, have been widely used to treat hypertension due to their unique properties that help to regulate blood pressure. The bioactive constituents, such as flavonoids, catechins, and theaflavins, present in these teas, possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce the risk of cardiovascular diseases associated with hypertension. Camellia sinensis teas help to treat hypertension by increasing the production of nitric oxide, which relaxes blood vessels and improves blood flow, thus lowering blood pressure. Regular consumption of Camellia sinensis teas has been shown to provide significant benefits in reducing the risk of cardiovascular diseases, improving overall cardiovascular health, and managing hypertension effectively.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 tablespoon of Camellia sinensis leaves (green tea or black tea) and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the leaves in boiling water for 3-5 minutes, then strain the tea into a cup.
Add 1/2 teaspoon of honey (optional) to the tea, as honey has natural sweetening properties.
Drink 1 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day, ideally in the morning and afternoon, to help lower blood pressure.
Consult a healthcare professional before using Camellia sinensis tea for hypertension, especially if taking medications or with pre-existing conditions.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Natural products and bioprospecting, Camellia sinensis teas for hypertension exhibit potential anti-hypertensive effects, although it is not specifically mentioned as a primary source of anti-hypertensive treatment in the provided study, which mainly highlights other herbs for their potential benefits.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Camellia sinensis teas can be an effective herbal preparation in treating hypertension, but possible side effects include dizziness, nausea, and rapid heartbeat due to the caffeine content, especially when consumed in excess or by individuals sensitive to its effects. When using Camellia sinensis teas to treat hypertension, it is essential to take precautions such as avoiding excessive consumption, maintaining a balanced diet, and staying hydrated to minimize the risk of side effects.

preparations.herb_desc: Hypericum perforatum teas, also known as St. John's Wort, have been used for centuries to treat various ailments, including hypertension. The bioactive constituents of this herbal preparation, such as hypericin and hyperforin, help to treat hypertension by exerting a vasodilatory effect, which in turn lowers blood pressure. The properties of Hypericum perforatum teas that aid in treating hypertension include their ability to reduce stress, improve mood, and prevent the formation of blood clots. Regular consumption of Hypericum perforatum teas has been found to provide significant benefits in managing hypertension, including reduced heart rate and blood pressure, and improved overall cardiovascular health.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Hypericum perforatum leaves.
Measure 1 teaspoon of dried leaves and place in a tea infuser.
Boil 1 cup of water in a pot and remove from heat.
Steep the dried leaves in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and drink 1-2 cups a day to help manage hypertension.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Hypericum perforatum teas can be used to treat hypertension, but it may cause side effects such as stomach upset, dizziness, and interactions with blood thinning medications. When using Hypericum perforatum teas to treat hypertension, it's essential to take precautions, such as monitoring blood pressure closely, avoiding excessive consumption, and being aware of potential allergic reactions.

preparations.herb_desc: Foeniculum vulgare teas, also known as fennel tea, have been traditionally used to treat hypertension due to their antihypertensive properties. The tea helps to lower blood pressure by reducing stress and anxiety, which are common contributors to hypertension. The bioactive constituents responsible for this effect include volatile oils such as anethole, fenchone, and limonene, which have been shown to have vasodilatory and antioxidant properties. Regular consumption of fennel tea has been found to provide benefits in reducing the risk of cardiovascular disease and improving overall cardiovascular health.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Foeniculum vulgare leaves and flowers, or 2 teaspoons of dried Foeniculum vulgare.
Add 1 cup of boiling water to a cup or teapot. Pour the boiling water over the Foeniculum vulgare leaves and flowers, or add the dried Foeniculum vulgare to the boiling water.
Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes. You can let it steep for a longer time if you prefer a stronger tea.
Strain the tea into a cup using a tea infuser or a piece of cheesecloth. Discard the Foeniculum vulgare leaves and flowers.

Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help manage hypertension. You can add honey or lemon to taste, but be sure to consult with a healthcare professional before making any changes to your diet or treatment plan.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Journal of ethnopharmacology, Foeniculum vulgare teas may offer a natural safeguard against the development of hypertension due to its reported diuretic effects, which include increasing urine excretion and urinary sodium excretion.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Foeniculum vulgare teas can be effective in treating hypertension, but its use may be associated with side effects such as gastrointestinal issues, including bloating, gas, and nausea, as well as allergic reactions like skin rashes and itching. To use Foeniculum vulgare teas safely, individuals should be cautious of its potential interactions with other medications, such as blood thinners, and consume it in moderation, avoiding excessive consumption to minimize the risk of adverse effects.

preparations.herb_desc: Curcuma longa teas, derived from the rhizomes of the turmeric plant, have been traditionally used to treat hypertension due to their bioactive constituents, including curcumin, demethoxycurcumin, and bisdemethoxycurcumin. These compounds possess potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce blood pressure by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and promoting the relaxation of blood vessels. The bioactive constituents in Curcuma longa teas also help to improve cardiovascular health by reducing oxidative stress and improving lipid profiles, thereby contributing to the overall management of hypertension. Regular consumption of Curcuma longa teas has been shown to provide benefits in reducing blood pressure, improving cardiovascular health, and reducing the risk of cardiovascular diseases associated with hypertension.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 teaspoon of dried Curcuma longa powder and 1 cup of boiling water.
Add the Curcuma longa powder to a cup and pour in the boiling water.
Let it steep for 5-7 minutes to allow the powder to infuse into the water.
Strain the mixture into another cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth.
Drink the tea immediately and repeat as needed, ideally 2-3 times a day.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Journal of complementary & integrative medicine, Curcuma longa teas for hypertension showed a cardioprotective effect by causing a decline in elevated blood pressure, reversing heart rate and QT segment reduction, and improving haematological and urinalysis parameters associated with hypertension.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Curcuma longa teas can be beneficial in treating hypertension, but possible side effects may include stomach upset, nausea, and diarrhea due to its high turmeric content. To minimize potential side effects, it is recommended to start with a low dose and gradually increase as needed, and also to be cautious when using it in combination with blood-thinning medications.

preparations.herb_desc: Humulus lupulus teas, also known as hops tea, have been traditionally used to treat hypertension due to their vasodilatory properties, which help to reduce blood pressure by relaxing the blood vessels and improving circulation. The bioactive constituents of hops tea, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, contribute to its therapeutic effects by exerting antioxidant and anti-inflammatory actions that help to mitigate the underlying causes of hypertension. Drinking hops tea has been found to help lower blood pressure by reducing the levels of stress hormones, such as cortisol, and promoting a sense of calmness and relaxation. The benefits of using hops tea to treat hypertension include its non-invasive and non-pharmacological nature, making it a popular choice for those seeking a natural and complementary approach to managing their blood pressure.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Humulus lupulus flowers. You can buy them from a health store or online.
Measure 1 tablespoon of dried flowers and place them in a tea infuser or a small muslin bag.
Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the flowers in the infuser or bag. Let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the flowers. Add honey or lemon to taste, if needed.
Drink 1-2 cups of the tea per day to help manage hypertension. Consult a doctor before making any changes to your diet or medication.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Humulus lupulus teas can be used to treat hypertension, but it may cause side effects such as dizziness, nausea, and stomach upset due to its potential interaction with blood thinning medications and its effect on blood pressure regulation. Precautions should be taken when using this herbal preparation to treat hypertension, such as drinking it in moderation, avoiding it with concurrent use of blood pressure medications, and being aware of potential allergic reactions like skin irritation or respiratory issues.

preparations.herb_desc: Avena sativa teas have been traditionally used to treat hypertension due to their vasodilatory properties, which help to relax blood vessels and improve blood flow, thereby reducing blood pressure. The bioactive constituents of Avena sativa, such as avenanthramides, avenacosides, and ferulic acid, contribute to its blood pressure-lowering effects by inhibiting the angiotensin-converting enzyme (ACE) and promoting nitric oxide production. The regular consumption of Avena sativa teas can lead to a decrease in systolic and diastolic blood pressure, thereby reducing the risk of cardiovascular complications. The benefits of using Avena sativa teas to treat hypertension include improved cardiovascular health, reduced risk of heart disease, and a decrease in the reliance on pharmaceutical medications.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Avena sativa (oat straw) and a tea infuser.
Boil 1 cup of water and let it cool for 1-2 minutes.
Place 1-2 tablespoons of dried Avena sativa in the tea infuser.
Steep the Avena sativa in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and drink 1 cup, 2-3 times a day to help manage hypertension.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Avena sativa teas can be used to treat hypertension, but possible side effects may include dizziness, lightheadedness, and changes in heart rate, which can be intensified in individuals with pre-existing heart conditions. To use Avena sativa teas safely, it is essential to start with low doses and gradually increase as needed, while also being cautious when consuming the tea on an empty stomach.

preparations.herb_desc: Astragalus membranaceus teas have been used in traditional Chinese medicine to treat hypertension due to their antihypertensive properties. The flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids present in this herbal preparation help to relax blood vessels, improve blood flow, and reduce blood pressure levels. The bioactive constituents, including astragaloside IV and calycosin, also exhibit vasodilatory effects that contribute to the reduction of blood pressure. Regular consumption of Astragalus membranaceus teas has been shown to provide benefits in managing hypertension, including improved cardiovascular health and reduced risk of cardiovascular complications.
preparation_procedure: Gather Astragalus membranaceus dried roots (about 2 tablespoons).
Combine the Astragalus roots with 1 cup of boiling water in a heat-resistant cup.
Let it steep for 5-7 minutes to allow the roots to release their medicinal properties.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into another cup. Discard the Astragalus roots.
Drink 1 cup of the Astragalus membranaceus tea, 2-3 times a day, to help manage hypertension.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Alternative medicine review : a journal of clinical therapeutic, Astragalus membranaceus teas have been found to have therapeutic benefit for the treatment of ischemic heart disease, myocardial infarction, heart failure, and relief of anginal pain, suggesting its potential use in managing hypertension.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Astragalus membranaceus teas can be beneficial in treating hypertension, but possible side effects include dizziness, fatigue, and changes in heart rate. When using Astragalus membranaceus teas to treat hypertension, it is essential to be cautious of interactions with other medications and to monitor blood pressure closely, as excessive consumption can lead to over-sedation or exacerbate the condition.
